serving.description: 1 set meal (rice bowl with chicken soboro, half-boiled egg, and miso soup or drink)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard set meal portion is approximately 500 g, comprising a bowl of Japanese rice topped with chicken soboro and mushroom sauce, one half-boiled egg, and a serving of miso soup or a drink.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Chicken, ground, cooked; White Rice, cooked; Egg, whole, cooked; Miso soup; composite estimation
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA or HPB entry exists for chicken soboro don combo as a composite dish. Per-100g values were derived by weighted-component estimation using USDA FoodData Central entries for cooked ground chicken (FDC ID 171477), cooked white short-grain rice (FDC ID 169756), whole cooked egg (FDC ID 173424), and miso (FDC ID 172443), with approximate component ratios consistent with a standard soboro don preparation (rice ~45%, ground chicken ~25%, egg ~10%, miso soup ~15%, mushrooms ~5%). Input values from the source record (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) were cross-checked against this USDA component estimate and retained where consistent. Sodium value of 276.9 mg/100g is plausible given soy sauce and miso contributions. All values are estimates; a laboratory analysis of the specific restaurant preparation would be required for high-confidence values.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database — white rice (Japanese short-grain, cooked)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 72 is based on the University of Sydney GI Database value for cooked Japanese short-grain white rice (GI ~72, glucose reference). The composite dish GI is dominated by the white rice component (~45% by weight). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (72 × 65.5) / 100 ≈ 47.3, where carbohydrate per 500g serving = 13.1 × 5 = 65.5g. The soboro sauce (mirin, sugar) and miso soup contribute additional glycaemic impact not captured in the rice-only GI estimate.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 276.9 mg/100g; estimated 1,385 mg per 500g serving from soy sauce, miso paste, and dashi stock
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced-sodium soy sauce and miso paste, ask for miso soup on the side to control portion, and avoid adding extra soy sauce at the table, as the estimated sodium per serving (≈1,385 mg) represents over 60% of the 2,000 mg daily limit recommended by the Singapore HPB.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI white rice (GI ~72) with 13.1g carbohydrate/100g and mirin/sugar in soboro sauce elevate postprandial blood glucose
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should request a brown rice substitution or a smaller rice portion to reduce glycaemic load (estimated GL ≈47 per standard serving), ask for soboro sauce on the side to limit mirin and sugar intake, and monitor postprandial glucose response given the high-GI rice base.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 1.2g/100g (6.0g per serving) and sodium 276.9mg/100g (≈1,385mg per serving) are the primary cardiovascular risk factors
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should be aware that the estimated sodium per serving exceeds 60% of the recommended daily limit and that the saturated fat contribution from ground chicken and egg yolk is moderate; choosing a smaller rice portion, requesting reduced-sodium condiments, and limiting egg yolk frequency are practical risk-reduction strategies.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Soboro Don Combo (鶏そぼろ丼コンボ) is a Japanese donburi set meal served in Singapore. The dish consists of a bowl of steamed Japanese short-grain white rice topped with finely ground chicken (soboro) and diced mushrooms simmered in a savory-sweet sauce made from soy sauce, mirin, sake, and sugar. The set is accompanied by a half-boiled egg and a choice of miso soup or a drink. The soboro topping has a moist, crumbly texture with a rich umami flavor balanced by mild sweetness from the mirin. The miso soup is prepared by dissolving fermented miso paste in dashi stock, adding a warm, savory complement to the meal. Nutritionally, the dish provides approximately 115 kcal per 100g, with 6.9g protein, 3.9g fat, and 13.1g carbohydrate. The sodium content is notable at 276.9mg/100g, primarily from soy sauce and miso, yielding an estimated 1,385mg per standard 500g serving. The white rice base carries a high glycaemic index (~72), making portion control important for individuals managing blood glucose. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those with soy, egg, gluten, or fish (dashi) allergies. It is a popular Japanese comfort food widely available at Japanese casual dining restaurants in Singapore.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for chicken soboro don combo as a composite restaurant dish. Per-100g values were estimated by weighted-component analysis using USDA FoodData Central entries for cooked ground chicken, cooked white short-grain rice, whole cooked egg, and miso, cross-checked against the input record (ai_generated, confidence 0.7). The input values are internally consistent and plausible. Medium confidence is assigned because the exact restaurant recipe, sauce ratios, and portion composition are not independently verified by a laboratory analysis or a primary food composition database entry.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite dish is not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2024 for the specific soboro don preparation. potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data for this composite dish are not available in a single approved source entry; component-level USDA data exists but composite-dish weighted values for these micronutrients were not computed due to insufficient recipe precision. Input source listed 'https://foodo.sg/' under HPB Singapore, but the URL does not resolve to a specific HPB nutrient record for this dish; the HPB source was therefore not used as primary nutrient evidence. Two portion entries in the input both marked is_default=true (500g and 650g); 500g was selected as the default serving size as it is the more conservative and commonly cited donburi set meal weight. The ethnic_tags in the input ('Beef Dish', 'Japanese Curry', 'Grilled Chicken Skewers') are clearly erroneous and have been discarded. trans_fat_g of 0.03g/100g is retained from the input as a trace value consistent with minor trans fat from animal-derived ingredients (chicken, egg); this is not a confirmed zero but a trace amount.
